author: afforai
description: Use Logically's AI Research Assistant to conduct citation-backed research on the files in your vault. - This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff.

manifest.author: Logically
manifest.description: Use Logically's AI Research Assistant to conduct citation-backed research on the files in your vault.
manifest.name: Logically AI Research Assistant

name: Logically AI Research Assistant

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### Functionality changes
- Cite your own notes - vault notes referenced inline in answers, like web/PDF sources
- Click a citation to open the note - opens in Obsidian immediately
- Wiki-link footnotes - proper `[[link]]`, opens right note even if two share a title
### Behind the scene
- Smarter use of your notes - summarized + trimmed, AI focuses on relevant parts, more room for web/academic results
- More reliable citation matching - number always maps to the right document
- Works with older plugin versions - graceful compatibility, nothing breaks mid-session
releases.name: 0.7.0 - Your vault notes, now used better in the conversation

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### Functionality Changes
- Research Assistant v2 — Upgraded core research engine with richer, multi-step reasoning and a visible thinking process
- Multi-tool search — Enable any combination of Library, Web, and Academic search simultaneously (all on by default), replacing the old single-mode dropdown
- Live thinking panel — See the assistant's reasoning in real time: which tools it chose, search progress, discovered sources, and a "Thought for N seconds" timer
- Suggested follow-up questions — After each answer, clickable follow-up suggestions to continue the conversation
- Connect Notes modal — New modal to browse, search, and select vault notes as context for every query (replaces the old file picker)
- Grouped sources — Sources now organized by type (Library / Academic / Web tabs) instead of a flat list
- Better error handling — Quota and backend errors now surface directly in the UI instead of failing silently
### Behind the Scenes
- Stream protocol upgraded to v2 with richer frame types
- Automatic settings migration from legacy search mode and context files
- New citation parsing with tool-type grouping
- Vault notes now inlined into prompts for reliable context delivery
- Removed deprecated `FilePicker` and `ModeSelector` components

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Functionality updates
- Fixed an issue where copying AI responses to your clipboard would silently fail when the Logically sidebar didn't have focus
- The copy button now shows a native Obsidian menu so you can choose to copy with or without citation footnotes, matching the feel of the rest of the app
- You now get a clear "Copied to clipboard" confirmation - or an honest "Failed to copy" notice if something went wrong
## Behind the scenes
- Replaced a custom-built copy popover with Obsidian's built-in Menu component, making the interaction smoother and more consistent
- Added a smarter clipboard utility that works reliably inside Obsidian's desktop environment by using the native system clipboard first, with a graceful fallback

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Functionality updates
- You can now use the plugin without signing in - get 3 free messages per day as a visitor
- A banner now appears in visitor mode with quick Sign in and Create account buttons
- You can now sign in via a modal overlay (e.g., when your daily limit is reached) without losing your current conversation
- When your daily free message limit is hit, you're now prompted to sign in or upgrade right in the chat
- Your conversation from visitor mode is now preserved when you sign in
- Sources in AI responses are now shown as a numbered list instead of bullet points
- The Settings panel now shows a Sign in prompt with options to create an account when you're not logged in
- Fixed an issue where an expired session silently failed - you'll now see a clear notification and be signed out cleanly
- Added a step-by-step installation guide for getting started with the beta
## Behind the scenes
- Authentication state is handled more reliably across all actions (model changes, message sending, regeneration)
- The default model is now loaded from a single shared config, making future updates more consistent
- Visitor requests are routed to a dedicated public endpoint, keeping authenticated and unauthenticated traffic separate
- Closing the login modal or clicking "Continue without logging in" now works smoothly without disrupting the UI

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Functionality updates
- AI model names are now up to date - Claude Sonnet 4.6, Claude Opus 4.6, and Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview are correctly shown everywhere
- The model name in chat messages now matches the model you actually selected, instead of sometimes showing an outdated name
- Upgrade modal now accurately reflects the latest models available on Pro and Enterprise plans
## Behind the scenes
- Chat display is now smarter about resolving model names based on your plan's available models
- Version updated to 0.3.0

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Functionality updates
- You can now see the latest available AI models automatically when you open the plugin - no need to update the plugin when new models are added
- Fixed an issue where the plugin would show confusing error messages when your login session expired
- The plugin now handles expired logins more gracefully - you'll simply see the login screen instead of error messages

## Behind the scenes
- Model selection now loads dynamically from the server, making it easier to add new AI models without plugin updates
- Authentication errors are now detected and handled more reliably
- Cleaned up diagnostic code that was slowing down the plugin
- Pages and interactions load more smoothly

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Functionality updates

- You can now research with AI directly inside Obsidian, using your own vault notes as source material
- Ask questions in three modes: search your vault documents, search the web with Google, or find academic papers with Semantic Scholar
- Get answers with inline citations and clickable sources - every claim is backed by a reference
- Choose from the latest AI models including OpenAI, Anthropic Claude, and Google Gemini
- Insert AI-generated answers directly into your active note with one click, complete with formatted footnotes and a sources section
- Drag and drop Markdown files from your vault into the assistant to add them as context
- Set custom instructions that apply to every conversation
- Switch between Logically accounts safely - your chat history clears automatically when you log in with a different account

## Behind the scenes

- Streamlined authentication with email/password or token-based login
- Secure API connection with verification tools built in
- Chat history persists when you log back into the same account
- File picker searches your entire vault to help you find relevant notes quickly
- The plugin works on both desktop and mobile Obsidian
